part. Made known, informed, taught, expounded.

part. Known, ascertained, comprehended, understood

s.m. A man completely versed in any science or śāstra.

def: adj. To be known or understood; to be investigated or inquired into; perceptible; conceivable; to be considered as.
s.m. A father; a paternal relation; a kinsman; a distant kinsman (one who does not participate in the oblations offered to deceased ancestors).
adj. & s.m. Knowing, wise, intelligent; acquainted (with); — one who knows, a knower, a wise or intelligent person; an acquaintance.

def: pron. Who, which, that, what
more: pron. rel. S. यः Pr. je . 1. Who; what; which; that. 2. (S. यदि ) conj. If. jo jo , adv. Whoever; whatever; whichever. jo chīz , What thing; whatever; whatsoever. jo kabhī , adv. If ever. jo kuchh ; S. यन्किञ्चित् Whatever; whatsoever; any. jo koī ; Bhoj. je keh ; S. यः कः Whoever; any. jo ki . Though; although ( agarche ); in asmuch as; whereas ( chūṅke ); seeing that. jo na lagtā ho, jo na miltā ho , What is inapplicable, or inappropriate. jo ho , Whatever. Jo ho so le āo. jo ho saktā hai . What is practicable or possible; what can be done. jo ho so ho . Come or happen what may.
(or jū ) 1. s. f. A river, a brook: see جوي joʼe. 2. part. act. (from جستن) used in compos. Seeking, seeker.

1. ( यःयद् &c.) pron. rel. Who, what, which, that. 2. or jau (for यदि ) conj. If. 3. jo or jau (for यदा ) adv. When: see جب jab. جوجو jo jo ( योयः ) Whoever, whatever. جوچيز jo chīz, What thing, whatever. جوکچهـ jo kuchh ( यत्किंचित् ) Whatever. جوکوٴي jo koʼī ( यःकोपि ) Whoever. جوکه jo-ki, Though, although, whereas, whichever. جولون jau-lauṅ, As long as: see جب jab.
subs: The thing which; whatever thing, whatever
Whatever happens; at all events, at any rate
Whatever can be done, what is practicable or possible

def: s.m. Youth, youthfulness, the prime of life, bloom of youth; puberty, manhood, adolescence; vigour, bloom, flourishing state; the pleasures of youth; exquisite enjoyment; the pleasures of sense, taste, or culture; (met.) the breast, well-developed breasts; a fine figure; beauty
more: n. m. S. यौवनम् Pr. jovvanam youth. See بـﮩـار 3, 4, 5. Baṛā joban ā rahā hai . 1. Puberty; adolescence; prime; youth; bloom. 1. Javānī meṅ gadhī ke bachche par bhī joban hotā hai . Prov. Even a donkey young is fair to see. 2. Kyā karūṅ morī māṅ? Morā naval jobanā yoṅ hī jāë; Lākh kahūṅ, morī mānat nāhīṅ, Jūṅ jūṅ manāūṅ voh to rūṅso hī jāë . Wom. Rus. song. O mother dear, what shall I do? My blooming youth is wasting so, Whate'er I say he 'll nothing heed, But angry gets the more I plead. 2. Well-developed breasts; a fine bust. Maiṅ kaise ke niksūṅ aṅ ganvā, Morā deorā nihāre jubanvā? Wom. Song. Thumrī. How can I in the area go! My brother-in-law stares at my bust so! 3. A fine figure; rounded charms; beauty. 1. Chhab gathṛī meṅ, joban rakābī meṅ . Prov. Fine show in dress, plumpness in the mess. 2. Nīm kā joban nīm nibaulī, ām kā joban aṅbyāṅ, Gorī kā joban pān phūl, panihāri kā joban k…
s. m. 1. Puberty, adolescence, prime, youth. 2. ( met. ) Breast.
subs: joban umaknā, joban umaṅdnā, v.n. Puberty to show itself; the breasts (of a woman) to be developed
To attain to puberty; to be in full vigour; to bloom
s.m. Youthful strength or vigour; — adj. Strong with the strength of youth
m. (joban-kī mātī, f.) adj. Intoxicated by (the pride of) youth
v.n. To taste or enjoy the pleasures of youth to the full, to revel in the pleasures of youth
m., joban-vatī, f. adj. Young, youthful, juvenile; attained to or arrived at the age of puberty, fit to be married, marriageable; beautiful, handsome.

def: s.m. = joban.
adj. S. योवनवत् Pr. jovanavanta youthful. 1. Young; youthful; juvenile. 2. Beautiful; handsome.
adj. f. At the age of puberty, fit to be married, youthful.
v.t. (prov.) = jovnā; johnā, qq.v. v.t. To see, look at, behold, regard.
s.m. A fighter, soldier, warrior, combatant; a brave fellow, a hero.
more: n. m. S. योध्दा A warrior; combatant; hero; champion; knight. 1. Sukh ke baṛe jodhā-rakhvālī haiṅ . Wom. Prov. The jewel happiness is guarded by great warriors bold. 2. Larat akāsh yugal sam jodhā, Ekāhīṅ ek hanat kari krodhā . Rāmāyan.

def: s.m. Fignting; battle.

ety: jodhā+Prk. प्पणं = त्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Soldiering; bravery, prowess, heroism.
s.m. A warrior &c. = jodhā.
s. f. A wife.
more: s. f. A rivulet, a brook. جويبار joʼebār, A large river formed of many smaller streams. ¶ jo-ī (from جستن) in compos. s. f. Seeking, searching.

def: n. f. S. जाया A wife. 1. Ghar meṅ āī joe, ṭeṛhī pagṛī sīdhī hoë . Prov. When the wife's brought home you then shall see The turban awry soon straightened will be. 2. Āsan driḍh, āhār driḍh, sumati gyān driḍh hoë; Tulsī! binā upāsnā bin dūlhe kī joë . Through meditation close and self command right mind will come, Tulsī! who worships not is as a bride without bridegroom. 3. Din kī bahan, rāt kī joë, Waqt paṛe mahtārī hoë . Riddle. Ans. A sword. A sister by your side, a wife by night, Provoke her and she is a mother fierce in fight.
s.f. A wife ( = jorū).

s.m. Joining, uniting, fitting; connection, union; yoking; putting to (horses); meeting, contact; mixture, combination, association; method, means, mode, manner; propriety, fitness, suitability; fixing (an arrow or other weapon); application of a remedy, remedy, cure; consequence, result; occasion, opportunity; (in Astron.) conjunction, a lucky conjuncture, auspicious or fortunate moment; (in Arith.) addition, sum, total; — application or concentration of the thoughts (keeping the body in a fixed posture); abstract contemplation, meditation, devotion, penance; union with the Supreme Being by means of abstract contemplation; the Yoga system of philosophy (as established by Patanjali, to teach the means by which the human soul may attain complete union with the Supreme Being); — the twenty-seventh part of a circle measured on the plane of the Ecliptic (and used in calculating the longitudes of the sun and moon); a division of the moon's path corresponding to the twenty-eight Nakshatras; (in banking) the person on whom a draft or bill of exchange is drawn; — adv. By means of, by reason of, through; in consequence of; on the part of, from
more: n. m. S. योग्य, Pr. joggo fit. 1. Conjunction of auspicious stars. 2. The nick of time; the auspicious moment. 3. Union with the Supreme being by means of abstract contemplation; religious meditation. Ye donoṅ jog sādhe baiṭhe hain . 4. A division of a great circle measured on the Ecliptic. 5. One in whose favor a bill is drawn. jog chāl , ( Ast .) Precession of the equinoxes. jog do-pahar chakkar , Equinoctial. jog sādhnā , v. n. To lead the life of an ascetic. jog lenā , v. n. To retire from the world; become a faqīr.
adv. On the part of ( taraf ); from.

adj. Fit; adequate; applicable; able; capable; proper; opportune; advisable. 1. Tan gudā-ke janam līyā, pī * bin rahī udās, Marne jogā kar dīyā, aisā kīyā masās . Riddle. Ans. Opium (or Love.) Double entendre . From body probed its birth, love-lorn (athirst) forlorn, To death's brink brought, by much attrition overborne. 2. Yeh muṅh pān jogā! Prov. Is this a mouth for pān? * 1. Drink. 2. A lover, spouse. † 1. Refuse of opium. 2. Like to. 3. Yeh marduā kis din kamāne jogā huā thā? Wom. When did this hulking fellow ever earn anything? jog-māyā , n. f. A goddess whose shrine is at the Kutab near Delhi.

s. m. ( योग ) 1. Junction, fitting. 2. A fortunate moment, opportunity, occasion. 3. Penance, devotion, religious meditation, union with the supreme being by means of abstract contemplation. 4. Certain divisions of a great circle, measured upon the Ecliptick. (See Asiat. Res. Vol. ix. p. 365). 5. ( योग्य ) adj. Possible, capable, fit. (In compos. it answers to able, worthy, &c.). جوگمايا jog-māyā, s. f. A deceptive power which Jogīs are supposed to possess.

s.f. The practice or cultivation of religious abstraction

s.f. The māyā or magical power of the Yoga, a certain illusionary power which Jogīs are supposed to possess (viz. the being able to assume at will any number of forms of their own body)
s.m. A kind of fish, Silurus ascita
s.f. A kind of sleep (supposed to be peculiar to devotees) which admits of the full exercise of the mental powers, a state of half-contemplation, half-sleep; light sleep, wakefulness
s.m. A menstruum or medium for mixing medicines; — s.f. Alkali; quicksilver

def: s.m. One who performs the kind of religious exercise called jog, a contemplative saint; a devotee, an ascetic, a hermit; one supposed to have obtained supernatural powers, a magician, a conjurer; — name of a particular mixed caste who are commonly weavers (this caste do not burn but bury their dead; and the women are sometimes buried alive with the corpses of their husbands).
more: n. m. S. योगी 1. A Hindu faqīr or ascetic; a devotee. ☞ Jogis do not burn, but bury their dead, and the women are sometimes buried alive with their husband's corpse. 1. Jogī terī sūnī manḍhyā kyoṅ? Rām Rām kare ram gaë jogī; dhūnī jūṅ kī tūṅ . Bhajan. O devotee! why is thy hut all still and silent now? With calling oft on Ram in Ram thy soul's absorbed, and lo! The sacred fire still burns as thou didst leave it here below! 2. Jogī jāt na pūchho, Rājah; Māran kūh, na gāran lājā , Padmāvat. Ask not, O King, of jogi's caste the name, Struck he's not hurt, abused he feels no shame. 2. A magician; conjuror.

s. m. 1. A Hindū faḳīr, a devotee. 2. A cast of Hindūs who are commonly weavers. (The people of this cast do not burn, but bury their dead; and, the women are sometimes buried alive with their husbandʼs corpse).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit योगिन् (yogin). Compare Punjabi ਜੋਗੀ (jogī), Bengali যোগী (jōgi), Marathi जोगी (jogī). Equivalent to جوگ (jog, “lit. union [with God], monastic renunciation, asceticism”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. The wife of a jogī; a female devotee or performer of jog.
more: E. n. f. The wife of a jogī. Nadī kināre dhuāṅ uṭhe, maiṅ jānūṅ kachhu hoë, Jā kāran jogan bhaī kahīṅ wohī na jaltā hoë . That rising smoke the river near—my heart misgives me sore, Not his the pyre sure for whom a Jogan I forlore!
s. f. Female of جوگي jogī, q. v.

def: s.f. A female demon or being of magical power (created by and attendant on Durgā), a witch, a sorceress; (in Astrol.) a spirit ruling periods of good and ill luck.
more: n. f. S. योगिनी 1. A female fiend or sprite, attendant on and created by Durga . 2. ( Astro .). Spirits ruling periods of good and ill-luck.
s. f. 1. A female fiend or sprite, attendant on and created by Durgā. 2. (in astrology) Spirits governing periods of good and ill luck.

def: s.f. (old H.) = joginī.
s.m. The colour worn by jogīs, a reddish-yellow colour; ochre (syn. geru); — name of a rāginī or musical mode; a kind of pigeon.
more: n. m. 1. Reddish yellow; ochre ( gerū ). Tore kāran, balamvā, chhāḍo des, ho kar līyo jogiyā bhes, Hāthoṅ bich sumran, gale bich selī, kāṅḍhe paṛe haiṅ kes . Wom. song. Birah. For you, my spouse, my home I've left, th' ascetic's garb put on, With beads in hand, black cord neck-hung, hair loose on shoulders flung. 2. A rāgnī or musical mode. Bajātī wo jogan jahāṅ jogiyā, To vāṅ baiṭhti khalq dhūnī lagā . Mīr Hasan. The world-renouncing maid upon her lyre played, A world attracted round, their sacred fires made.
s. m. 1. Name of a colour. 2. f. The name of a rāginī or musical mode. 3. A kind of pigeon.

def: v.t. To salute, to address reverentially, to make obeisance to.
adv. S. यहि Correl. of vohīṅ . As soon as; the very moment; no sooner. Johīṅ kahā, vohīṅ kīyā . No sooner said than done.

ety: joh˚ = Prk. जोएह(इ), or जोअ(इ), or जोए(इ); S. ज्योत्स्नय(ति), fr. ज्योत्स्न

def: v. a. To look out for ( intizār k ).

s. m. A measure of four cos (کوس kos, q. v.).

def: n. f. 1. Risk; hazard; stake; venture. 1. Kyoṅ kampo, kyoṅ thartharo, dhīraj kyoṅ na dharo? Dohā. Chākhā chāho pirem ras, jokhoṅ kyoṅ na saho? Why shake and tremble so? a stout heart you must bear, If love's sweets you would taste its danger you must share. 2. Kānī ke biyāh ko sau jokhoṅ . Prov. In marrying a one-eyed girl there are a hundred risks. 2. Fear; danger; jeopardy. 3. Any valuables, as jewels, money, etc. 4. Insurance ( bīmā ). jokhoṅ uṭhānā , v. n. To suffer loss. jokhoṅ kā kām , n. m. A dangerous affair; a hazardous enterprise. jokhoṅ meṅ paṛnā , v. n. To run a risk. jokhoṅ meṅ ḍālnā , v. n. To risk; endanger.
v.t. = jokhnā, q.v. v.t. To weigh; to measure; to estimate, rate, take the measure or amount of (one's abilities, &c.).

n. m. H. jāl net; Z. and P. jolāh ; A. julāhiq . 1. A weaver ( kolī ). 1. Julāhe kī jūtī, sipāhī kī joë, dharī dharī purānī hoë . Prov. A soldier's wife, a weaver's shoes, alike grow old for lack of use. 2. Āṭh julāhe, nau hukkā, tis par bhī thukkam thukkā . Prov. Nine hookkas 'mong eight weavers, see A hookkah fight amongst them free. ☞ Weavers are proverbially stupid. One story is that a crow, perched on the housetop, having carried off his child's bread, before giving him some more he took the precaution to remove the ladder. Another story is that, being told by a Mah. soothsayer ( rammāl ) it was written in his fate that his nose would be cut off by an axe, the simple weaver was incredulous, and, taking up an axe he kept moving it saying—' Yūṅ karbā to goṛ kaṭbā, yoṅ karbā to hāth kaṭbā, aur yoṅ karbā tab nā '—'If I do so I cut my leg, if I do so I cut my hand, but unless I do so—(His nose was off). 3. Jolhā jāne jau kāṭe? Can a weaver cut barley? ☞ The story goes that a weaver, unable to pay his debt, was set to cut the ripe barley by his agriculturist creditor who thought to repay himself in this way. Instead of reaping he was continually untwisting the tangled barley stems. 4. Jolhā chorābathi narī narī, Khodā chorābathi ekke berī . Tir. Prov. The weaver steals a reel at a time, God has him robbed at one swoop (whole pieces of cloth). 2. A fool; blockhead. 3. An aquatic insect. julāhe kī sī dāṛhī , n. f. A short, pointed French beard, like a weaver's. chamar-julāhā , W.; tāṅtī julāhā , E. n. m. An inferior caste of weavers.
s.f. Partner, fellow, companion, &c. = joṛī, q.v. (used chiefly in comp, e.g. ham-jolī, q.v.).

Contrivance, arrangement, mechanism, joint, joining: see, also, جوڙ جاڙ.توڙ joṛ-jāṛ, s. m. Savings, collecting by small quantities, scraping together.

def: adj. = zorāwar.
more: n. m. S. जुड to join. 1. A join; joining. 2. A connecting ring. ( chhallā ). 3. A joint ( joṛ-band ). Ikke kī savārī meṅ joṛ joṛ hil jātā hai . 4. A patch; seam. 5. Soldering. Joṛ khul gayā . 6. A pair; match ( joṛā ). Apne joṛ se laṛo! Hit one of your own size. 7. Conjunction; connexion; combination. 8. Sum; total; ( Arith .) addition. 9. ( Math .) Plus (A. musbat , S. dhun ). 10. A made-up story; an ingenious or imposing tale. 11. A plot; trick. Achchhā joṛ chale. joṛ biṭhānā , v. a. To dovetail.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ज्वर (jvará).

ety: jor˚ = joṛ˚, rt. of joṛnā, q.v.+S. उ+का
def: s.f. A wife
more: n. f. S. जाया, H. joṛā match. A wife; consort. 1. Ghar meṅ jorū kā nām bahū Begam rakh lo . Prov. Every man may call his wife a queen. 2. Jorū ne parvānā bhejā, 'laṛke bhūke marte haiṅ.' 'Laṛke gaë padar ke charkhe, ham to maujeṅ karte haiṅ! ' Song. Ṭesū. The wife a letter sent, 'the children starve for want of bread,' 'Why let the children go to pot, I am with pleasure fed.' jorū kā mazdūr, gulām, yā murīd , n. m. A henpecked husband; a slave to one's wife; an uxorious husband. Kuchh na bole jo chhināle ko bhī sir par rakh le, Mard kyā! jorū kā mazdūr woh bhaṛvā hogā . Wom. Rāhat. His wife may play the adultress, not a word says he, Call him a man? her slave he's shewn himself to be. jorū kā bhāī , n. m. ( Slang ). Brother-in-law ( sālā ). Sārī Khudāī ek taraf, jorū kā bhāī ek taraf . Prov. The world on one side, the wife's brother on the other. (Please the wife please her brother).
subs: jorū-kā mazdūr, jorū-kā murīd, s.m. A wife's slave, an uxorious husband; a hen-pecked husband.

def: s.m. Joining, connection; a pair, couple; one of a pair, fellow, mate, match, counterpart; partner, consort (wife or husband); a pair of shoes; a suit (of clothes); — alchemy; alloy
more: s. m. 1. Joining, connexion, a pair, a couple; a pair of shoes; a suit (of clothes). 2. Alchymy.
subs: v.n. To form or mix alloy, to alloy.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀚𑁄𑀟 (joḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-); see there for more.

def: n. m. joṛī , n. f. 1. Counterpart; pair; mate; match. 1. Rām milāī joṛī, ek andhā, ek koṛhī . Phabtī. Chaff. Rām hath this rare match made, a leper to a blind one wed. 2. Sirhāne tere mātā rove, pāṅyat rove gorī, Bhujā pakaṛ-kar bhāī rove 'āj bichhaṛ gaī joṛī! ' Kabīr. Your mother at your head-side weeps, your wife weeps at your feet, Your brother holds your arm and weeps 'my mate no more I'll meet.' 3. Phāgun hai sakhī, raṅg mahīnā, bichhṛī āë mile joṛī . Wom. Holī song. In February, dear, the bright-hued month, my parted mate to me returned. 4. Andhā sipāhī, kānī ghoṛī, Bidhnā ne āp milāī joṛī . Prov. A soldier blind on a one-eyed mare, the Power Supreme hath bound this pair. 5. Joṛā dilvāo . Faqīrs. Give me two pice. 6. Achchhī joṛī milī . He has found his match. 3. A pair of shoes. 4. A suit of clothes. 5. Consort; wife or husband. joṛā banānā , v. n. To alloy; mix alloy. joṛ toṛ , n. m. Contrivance; device; craft ( udheṛbun ). joṛtoṛ k . To contrive; devise; exercise one's skill. 2. To plot against. joṛ jāṛ , n. m. Small savings; scraps. joṛ joṛ . Every joint of the body. jūṛiyā sanjog , Conjunction of auspicious stars. Joṛiyā sanjog ke hāth hai . Prov. When stars propitious bend a mate for you they'll send. (Good fate good mate). juṛā-huā , adj. Joined; united; attached; appended. ghoṛā joṛā , A present of a horse and a khilat .
more: n. m. 1. A top-knot. 2. The crest or plume of a nightingale. 3. A rope of twisted grass or twine, made to support a round-bottomed jar. 4. The hinder part of a turban.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Joined; mended; patched, &c.

s.m. Joining; collecting, &c.; that which joins or binds, &c.; solder; rennet
subs: s.m. One who joins or mends, &c., a joiner, a mender; one who collects or amasses, &c.; one who adds or sums up, &c. (see joṛnā).

def: s.f. A pair, couple; a fellow, assessor, mate; partner, consort; match, equal, counterpart; — a carriage and pair; (local) a pond, tank; — adj. fem. of joṛā, q.v.

ety: joṛ(nā)+Prk. अडओ; S. अ+र or ल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Twin; (in Math. & Bot.) conjugate; — a twin
n. A twin; twin brother. joṛlā , adj. ( Bot .) Conjugate.
n. ( Math .) Componendo. joṛ lagānā , v. a. 1. To sum up; add. 2. To patch. joṛ'ī, joṛiyā . Connote.
v.t. = joṛnā, q.v.
more: v. a. S. जोड़न 1. To join; unite; connect; put together. 2. To add; sum up; total. 3. To calculate; reckon; work a sum. 4. To bring, get, or gather together; to collect; assemble; group. Āp hī khiyālī, āp hī mālī, kalī kalī kar joṛe hai, Kachchī pakkī kī sār na jāne, man māne phal toṛe hai . Himself Creator, gardener too, young buds he heaps a store, 'Twixt green and ripe he knows not aught, but plucks all in his power. 5. To scrape together; save up; lay by; amass; accumulate. 1. Ākhir maroge, rupayā joṛ joṛ kyā karoge.? Prov. One day you are to die, why riches then lay by. 2. Joṛ joṛ mar jāëṅge, aur māl jaṅvāī khāëṅge . Prov. The riches you heap up and die, your son-in-law will sole enjoy. 3. Māyā kā kyā joṛnā, khal khānī, kambal oṛhnā . Prov. Who riches heap from day to day on self but prey, in sackcloth lay. 4. Joṛ joṛ-ke dharī dharan meṅ, hai yeh jis tis kī, Samajh, nar, hai māyā kis…

ety: joṛ˚ = Prk. जोड(इ) or जोडे(इ) = S. जोटय(ति) rt. जुट्
def: v.t. To join, unite, connect, bind, tie, attach (to, -se); to mend, patch; to cement, solder; to stitch together; to set (a bone, &c.); to bind up, heal, or comfort (a broken heart, &c.; e.g. ṭuṭe dil-kā joṛnā); to yoke; to harness ( = jotnā); to match, mate, pair; to add together, cast up, sum up; to reckon, calculate, compute; to bring or get together, to collect, to assemble; to accumulate, amass, to scrape together, to save up; to consolidate; to coagulate; to compose (verses, &c.); to make up (a fire); to brighten up, to light (a lamp); to contrive, scheme, devise, plan; to invent, fabricate, concoct
more: v. a. 1. To join, to mend, to patch. 2. To add together. 3. To reckon, to cast or sum or add up. 4. To get or rather to save and scrape up a fortune, &c. to fabricate, cement, solder, unite, set (a bone, &c.), to tie, annex, affix, invent, contrive.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀚𑁄𑀟𑁂𑀇 (joḍei); see there for more.

def: s.f. Counting, adding; calculation, computation, reckoning; the sum total.
E. n. f. 1. Calculation; reckoning. 2. The sum total.

def: s.m.f. A twin; a pair, mate, companion, consort (see juṛwāṅ).
more: adj. (dakh.) Equal, peer: see هم سر ham-sar.

ety: prob. Zend yukhsh, fr. yuz = S. युज्
def: s.m. Boiling, ebullition; effervescence; heat, excitement, passion, emotion; lust; fervour, ardour, zeal; vehemence; enthusiasm; frenzy
more: n. m. 1. Boiling; ebullition; 2. Emotion; excitement; passion; lust. Tum ko baṛā josh āyā . 3. Zeal; ardour; vehemence; enthusiasm; fanaticism ( taassub ). 4. Poetic frenzy. josh-i-jāvanī , v. n. The fire or vivacity of youth. josh kharosh , n. 1. Roar; thunder; din. 2. Anger; passion; rage; fret and fume. josh-i-khūn , n. m. 1. Full-bloodedness; plethora . 2. Maternal or fraternal affection; ties of blood. josh-i-dahan ; H. mūnh ānā , A sore mouth. josh denā , v. a. To boil. josh khānā, yā mārnā , v. n. To boil up. josh meṅ ānā , v. n. 1. To swell; bubble up; overflow. Daryā josh meṅ ā rahā hai . 2. To be enraged, etc. 3. To be touched, moved, overcome with emotion. 4. To be heated; to feel the sexual passion. 5. To be inspired. josh meṅ lānā , v. a. To enrage, excite, etc.

1. s. m. Ebullition, effervescence; heat, passion, lust, emotion. جوش خروش josh ḵẖarosh, s. m. Anger, passion, rage. جوش خون josh-i-ḵẖūn, s. m. Ebullition of the blood (the name of a disease). جوش دينا josh-denā, To cause to boil, to boil. جوش زن josh-zan or جوش زنان josh-zanān, adj. Boiling up, in a state of ebullition. جوش کهانا josh khānā, or جوش مارنا josh mārnā, To boil or bubble up. 2. (In compos.) part. act. (from جوشيدن) Boiling, effervescing.
subs: s.m. The ardour or fire of youth
josh-o-ḵẖarosh, s.m. Noise, tumult, din; excitement, working of passion, anger, passion, rage
s.m. Ebullition of the blood; full-bloodedness, plethora; maternal or fraternal affection
s.m. A breaking out or eruption of the mouth
(-ko), To cause to boil, to boil
josh-zanān, adj. Boiling up, in a state of ebullition
v.n. To boil, to bubble up

josh-meṅ ānā, v.n. To begin to boil, to boil, bubble up, to ferment; to swell, overflow; to chafe, rage; to be heated; to be agitated (as the waves of the sea); to be lustful; to be moved, be touched, be overcome with emotion; to be in a frenzy; to be inspired
v.t To rouse one's anger, &c.; to enrage, excite, &c. (see josh).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جوش (jōš).

def: part. (of joshīdan; see josh), Boiling, bubbling up; foaming (as waves).

def: s.f. def: s.m. A pair, couple, brace; a term used in the game of ćausar when two or more pieces come together on the same square (opp. of a blot).
more: n. m. S. युग Pr. juggam . 1. An age; epoch; æra. (The ages are four— Satyā, Tretā, Dwāpar and Kali ). Hameṅ to yāṅ jug bīt gaë . 2. A period of 12 or 24 years ( qaran ); hence, a very long period. ' Chalte chalte jug huā, kitnī dūr hai gāoṅ?' 'Nāoṅ na jānūṅ, bhekjī, paun kos hai gāoṅ .' Dohā. 'I've toiled and toiled, an age I've spent, how far's the hamlet hence?' 'Its name, O devotee, unknown, it might be two miles hence.' 3. Two pieces coming together in the same square. ( Chausar ). Jug-tūṭā, nard mārī . Prov. The pair divided, the piece slain. (Divide and conquer). 4. A thread. (Brocade-workers). Goṭe meṅ jug ḍāl do. jug phoṛnā , v. a. See phūṭ ḍālnā, jug jug , adv. Always; for ever. Jug jug, jug jug jīyā karo, dūdh batāshe piyā karo! Menḍicants' cry. Live, live a thousand thousand years, on milk and sugar live through all these years!

s. m. 1. An age of the world or great period. (The ages are four, i. e. Satya or Kr̤ita, Tretā, Dwāpar and Kali ). 2. A term used in the game of chausar (the opposite of a blot, two or more men standing on the same square). 3. A pair. جگ پهوڙنا يا پهوٿنا jug-phoṛnā or -phūṭnā ( युगस्फोटन ) v. a. To cause a difference between friends, to fall out. جگانجگ jugānjug or jugānujug ( युगानुयुग ) Ages of ages, from age to age, for ages. جگ جگ jug jug, adv. Constantly, eternally.
wety: Borrowed from English jug.

def: s.m. A yoke; — an age, a period, an epoch, a cycle; an astronomical cycle of five years, a lustrum; (in Myth.) an age of the world, a long mundane period of years (of which there are four, viz. def: s.m. A mixture of syrup and rose-water; a sherbet of sugar and rose-water; — a purgative (syn. mus-hil, the ordinary meaning of the word in India).

s. m. A purge. (q. d. Drawing, from جلب Drew; and, in this sense, it is generally used in India: but, Golius says) A mixture of water and sirup ( perfumed with rosewater, from Pers. گل A rose, and آب Water); hence, julap.

def: s.f. 'Friday-eve,' i.e. Thursday.

'Twas ever thus, in boyhood too hath pleasure fled away, The dread of school next day e'er spoiled the Friday's holiday. 2. The Friday prayer. Kaho jumā kahaṅ paṛhā . 3. W. An arena; gymkhāna. Chalo be! jumme meṅ chal-kar kushtī dekheṅ .
s. m. Friday (on which day Musalmāns assemble to pray at the great mosque).

def: s.f. 'Friday-eve'; Thursday
more: n. f. Thursday ( birhaspat ). The graves and shrines of holy men are lighted up and offerings made on this day. 1. Jumerāt pīroṅ kī karamāt . Thursday's the day Saints miracles display. 2. Kyoṅ bābā, jumerāt kā chirāgā milegā? Mah. Mendicants' cry. Will you give me, Sir, the wherewithal the Thursday's lamp to light? 3. Hai jumerāt, ghar apne ko dugānā mat jā, Āj de ḍāl yahīṅ Lāl Parī kī baiṭhak . Wom. It's Thursday, love, to your own house dont go, Your offering here on Lāl parī bestow! 4. Jumerāt merī dāī, jumme kī khabar lāī, Hafta merā nānā, aur kān pakar bulānā . Nursery rhyme. The Thursday is my nurse who brings me news of Friday, holiday, My dad is Saturday who takes me by the ear to school away. kālī jumerāt , n. f. lit. black Thursday. Doomsday. Kālī jumerāt ko ānā .
wety: First attested in c. 1669 as Middle Hindi جمعرات (jm'rat /⁠juma'rāt⁠/), a contraction of جُمْعَہ رات (jm'h rat), from جُمْعَہ (jum'a, “Friday”) + رات (rāt, “eve”), literally “Friday eve”, because as per Islamic and Abrahamic tradition, the day changes at sunset.

def: s. f. Thursday.

def: n. f. S. यूका A louse. 1. Koṛhī ke jūṅ nahīṅ paṛtī . Prov. Lice like not lepers. 2. Jūoṅ ke ḍar se gudṛī nahīṅ pheṅkī jātī . Prov. Who ever threw away his blanket for the lice in it? jūṅ dekhnā , v. a. To pick out the lice in one's hair. jūṅ kī chāl , n. f. Very slow or creeping motion. jūṅ kī chāl chalnā , v. n. To creep along. jūṅ-muṅhā , adj. Smooth-tongued; hypocritical. jūëṅ mārnā , v. n. To dawdle; faddle. kān pe jūṅ na chalnā , v. n. Not to feel, heed, or be affected; to turn a deaf ear. Lākh kahā, par iske kān par ek jūṅ nahīṅ chaltī. marī jūṅ , n. f. lit . a dead louse. 1. A slow heavy person. 2. A sluggard; drone. jam jūṅ , n. f. A body louse.
more: adv. S. यथा Pr. jahā . As; joṅ toṅ, joṅ toṅ kar-ke , adv. 1. In some way or other; any how. 2. With great difficulty or hardship. Parī sāth kāṭī woh joṅ toṅ ke rāt, Uṭhā subah maltā huā apne hāth . Mīr Hasan. joṅ joṅ, jūṅ jūṅ ; Bhoj. jav jav , adv. Correl. of voṅ voṅ . As far as; as long as. 1. Joṅ joṅ minnat karo, voṅ voṅ sir par chaṛhtī hai . 2. Joṅ joṅ chiṛyā moṭī ho, voṅ voṅ dum sukeṛe . Prov. As the bird fattens his tail shrinks. (As riches grow frugality doth show). joṅ kā toṅ, jūṅ kī tūṅ ; Bhoj. jeṅv ke teṅv , adv. Exactly; as it was; neither more nor less ( ṭhīk , adv. 1, 2, 3.) joṅ kā toṅ, jūṅ kī tūṅ , adj. Perfect; whole; sound; unbroken; untouched; undiminished; same; the very same ( ṭhīk , adj. 3). Phr. The very thing; the real thing. joṅ kā toṅ rakhnā , v. n. 1. To let be; to keep safe and sound. 2. To uphold; maintain. joṅ hīṅ , adv. No sooner; instantly.
